context: Tests that the agent detects hardcoded AWS credentials and Azure subscription ID in variables, a :latest container tag, and an SSL bypass (curl -k), then produces a corrected pipeline and a structured security report.

checklist.name: Hardcoded AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID detected
checklist.description: security-report.md (or equivalent) flags the AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID variable value as a hardcoded credential finding

checklist.name: Hardcoded AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY detected
checklist.description: security-report.md flags the AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY variable value as a hardcoded credential finding

checklist.name: Container :latest tag detected
checklist.description: security-report.md flags node:latest as an unpinned container image

checklist.name: SSL bypass (curl -k) detected
checklist.description: security-report.md flags the curl -k flag as a TLS/SSL verification bypass

checklist.name: Credentials replaced with variable group or secret references
checklist.description: In the corrected azure-pipelines.yml, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY are replaced with $(VAR_NAME) references or removed from the variables block — no literal key values remain

checklist.name: Container image pinned
checklist.description: In the corrected azure-pipelines.yml, the container image reference no longer uses :latest — it specifies a concrete version tag or SHA digest

checklist.name: SSL bypass remediated
checklist.description: In the corrected azure-pipelines.yml, -k is removed from the curl command (or the step is updated to use TLS verification)

checklist.name: Severity levels assigned
checklist.description: security-report.md assigns a severity level (HIGH, MEDIUM, or INFO) to each finding

checklist.name: Risk explanation provided
checklist.description: security-report.md includes a brief explanation of the risk for at least two of the findings (e.g., key exposure, unpinned images, MITM)
